childMarkdownRemark.rawMarkdownBody:
Hello! 👋 my name is Bailey and I am a **junior** studying **computer science** at the **University of Illinois**. My main interests are in application backend development, applied machine learning, and industry scale quantum computing! 

***I am currently seeking a Summer 2020 software engineering internship opportunity!***

# Okay, but why quantum?

I originally started college as a physics major and fell in love with the unintuitiveness of quantum physics. Except, I still wanted to work on projects that could have clear, meaningful impact on day-to-day lives--which would not have been very tangible as a quantum physicist. So, even though I made the switch to computer science, I still kept true to my roots and took up interest in quantum computing. 

# How did you get involved then?

There was one problem with pursuing quantum computing; my university offered effectively no courses or opportunities for undergraduates in the space. I knew that I had to have tons of peers just as interested as I was, but without any outlet to find them I had no idea where to even start. Simply put, I wanted to learn more, but I knew it would not be easy doing so alone. So rather than just giving up or waiting around for something to happen, I created [QUIUC](https://www.quiuc.org). I may not have been an expert on quantum computing, but I knew that if I brought the right people together I could learn from their experiences, as well as teach some of my own. The turnout far exceeded any of my initial aspirations. In just a few short months we scaled from nothing to over 150 freshmen through graduate students across physics, computer science, and engineering. Not only are we teaching workshops to get new members going, but we are also hacking on research projects together and forming partnerships with professors and industry leaders to bring our members to the bleeding edge of QIS research.  

# So what do you want to do with quantum?

Though I am excited for its potential, I also recognize that there is a long road ahead before the promised exponential speed-ups in medical research and AI will be within grasp 🚀. As I had mentioned before, I am an engineer at the end of the day. So until quantum computing can be fully realized, I have found my place in application backend development. In particular, the subtle improvements machine learning can make that effect the backbone of our modern lives.

# Well, what else are you interested in?

Aside from QUIUC, I also lead the [Association of Data Science and Analytics](http://adsa.cs.illinois.edu) 📊, work on my new social media app 📱, *Overheard*, with my roommate, and research 📚 educational tools under Professor Geoffrey Challen for [CS 125](cs125.cs.illinois.edu).

During my free time, I am an amateur road cyclist 🚲 and love cooking 🥘 with my friends and family.
